text.title: Junior AI Co-Pilot Curriculum Failure Modes
text.summary: Five recurring failure modes in junior AI onboarding programs with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.description: Vibes-only training — junior watches a 30-min recording, no exercises, no review.
antipattern.symptom: First week PRs include obvious hallucinated APIs and missing tests.
antipattern.root-cause: Watching transfers near-zero skill; without exercise the junior cannot recognise their own mistakes.

antipattern.description: No gating rubric — anyone who finishes the calendar slots "graduates".
antipattern.symptom: Juniors with very different competence levels emerge from the same program; one becomes a force multiplier, another causes incidents.
antipattern.root-cause: Time-based completion measures attendance, not competence.
antipattern.fix: Binary rubric (≥10 items) with evidence pointer per item; no graduation without 100% pass (rule r5).

antipattern.description: No mentor — junior runs the curriculum solo, asks Slack for help.
antipattern.symptom: Slack-help quality varies wildly; junior absorbs whoever happened to answer (sometimes another junior).
antipattern.root-cause: Mentor cost not budgeted; "junior can self-study" optimism.
antipattern.fix: Named mentor + weekly 30-min calendar block (rule r4).

antipattern.description: Single-tool tunnel — curriculum hard-codes one co-pilot and junior never learns to compare alternatives.
antipattern.symptom: Junior is helpless when the team switches tools or when one model is degraded.
antipattern.root-cause: Curriculum was written by the tool vendor or evangelist.
antipattern.fix: One primary tool, but include a "compare-against-alternative" exercise in the prompt-anatomy module.

antipattern.description: No security module — IPI, secrets-in-prompts, tool allow-lists never taught.
antipattern.symptom: Junior pastes prod creds into a prompt; cred ends up in a tool-vendor log.
antipattern.root-cause: Security treated as DevOps concern, not as a co-pilot usage skill.
antipattern.fix: Required security-boundaries module with IPI + secrets + allow-list exercises (rule r6).
